name: codex-qa
tagline: QA harness for the omo Codex plugin: isolated CODEX_HOME + mock model, real ~/.codex and real API calls never touched.
verdict: Solid, well-isolated QA skill: mock model (no real API calls), isolated/mktemp CODEX_HOME, shasum checks proving the real ~/.codex is untouched, self-testing scripts, deterministic evidence capture. Minor gaps only: no explicit 'do not use for X' guidance and thin failure-recovery notes, neither posing risk to a user who installs it.

highlights.summary: Docker QA path says 'a disposable container that has the latest codex' (script/agent/qa-docker.sh) rather than a pinned version, unlike the pinned 'codex-cli 0.140.0' used for local scripts — minor dr
No explicit negative-trigger statement (e.g. 'not for QA of a user's real ~/.codex or unrelated Codex work') beyond implied scope.
Failure modes are named (e.g. 'codex hard-errors' without CODEX_HOME) but no explicit recovery/retry guidance is given for script failures.
